mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations, causing equity markets to price in lower corporate revenues and negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in greater than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of accelerating CPIAUCSL and rising FEDFUNDS creates a stagflationary pricing environment where real discount rates increase while nominal growth expectations fall, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS shows no predictive power with coefficient p-value above 0.05 in multivariate regression, or forward returns are positive when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary easing, a monthly increase in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate output, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during easing cycles fail to predict positive 5-day returns, or the predictive coefficient flips negative in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real personal income growth directly increases household purchasing power and corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as equity valuations adjust to stronger earnings expectations.
failure_condition: RSAFS increases are followed by negative 20-day forward returns in greater than 60% of observations, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment surge exceeding 200,000 signals robust economic expansion and strong labor demand, which boosts consumer confidence and corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by > 200k in a single month
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in greater than 55% of months where PAYEMS exceeds the 200k threshold, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for prior trend growth.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and real GDP indicate synchronized strength in residential investment and aggregate demand, which amplifies corporate earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint movement of HOUST and GDPC1 shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models, or forward returns are negative when both series expand concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, monthly CPIAUCSL spikes trigger immediate algorithmic risk-off positioning and margin calls, causing sharp negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as investors demand higher risk premiums.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases during high-inflation periods fail to predict negative 5-day returns, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.
